input.prompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

input.initialPrompt:
I'm building a chat messaging screen in .NET MAUI targeting .NET 10. When the
keyboard opens, it covers my text input at the bottom of the screen. In .NET 9
I used WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize but that doesn't seem to work anymore.
How do I make the input bar move up when the keyboard appears?

data.reasoningText:
The user is asking about a .NET MAUI issue where the keyboard covers the text input at the bottom of the screen. They mention that `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` worked in .NET 9 but doesn't work in .NET 10.

This is a known change in .NET MAUI. In .NET 10 (which targets Android API 35+), edge-to-edge display is enforced, and the old `WindowSoftInputModeAdjust.Resize` approach was deprecated. The new approach uses `WindowInsets` and the `Ime` inset type.

Let me provide a concise answer about how to handle this in .NET 10.


In .NET 10 targeting Android API 35+, the old resize approach is no longer supported due to edge-to-edge display enforcement. Instead, I need to use the `WindowInsets` API to listen for keyboard insets and adjust the view's padding dynamically based on IME visibility.
